definition: EM carry index providers drop countries after capital control events and restructurings, creating selection bias that inflates historical returns by 2-4% p.a. A full-inclusion EM carry index reduces the Sharpe ratio from 0.5-0.7 to 0.2-0.4, a material correction to the standard evidence base for EM carry attractiveness.
evidence: Argentina, Russia, Egypt examples of post-event index exclusion
Full-inclusion index Sharpe 0.2-0.4 vs standard index Sharpe 0.5-0.7
Not directly contested in any debate
Sequential orthogonalization on 6-currency EM basket (2005-2025, n=240): DXY R²=47%, EMBI credit incremental R²=23%, residual 30%
Survivorship bias examples: Argentina, Russia, Egypt capital control events excluded from indices reduce full-sample Sharpe from 0.5-0.7 to 0.2-0.4
open_questions: Is the 2-4% magnitude robust to different inclusion criteria and crisis-period pricing assumptions?
Does DM carry also suffer from survivorship bias (e.g., exclusion of countries that adopted the euro)?
Decomposition sensitivity to currency inclusion (TRY vs MXN dominate results)
Whether the 6-currency basket is representative of the broader EM carry opportunity set
